context.taskDescription: Operational Hardening v2 — 5 phases (Foundation, Instrumentation, Operational Intelligence, Visual Observability, Optimization) + 9 post-test improvements + anti-fingerprint hardening
completedSteps: Phase 1: Foundation (5 tasks) — dead code cleanup, Zod config, SenderHealth SQLite, WorkerOrchestrator extraction, integration tests
Phase 2: Instrumentation (4 tasks) — EventRecorder message trace, AuditLogger, Prometheus metrics (prom-client), event enrichment + sender lifecycle events
Phase 3: Operational Intelligence (4 tasks) — SendWindow (business hours), SenderWarmup (4-tier progressive), Pause/Resume sender, DeviceCircuitBreaker
Phase 4: Visual Observability (3 tasks) — SenderDashboard UI, Grafana embed tab, toast alerts + StatsBar indicators
Phase 5: Optimization (4 tasks) — ScreenshotPolicy (sampling/JPEG/retention), ContactCache (TTL), OptOutDetector + blacklist, MediaSender (share intent)
Post-implementation review: fixed 4 issues (Prometheus enrichment labels, orphaned metrics, screenshot content-type, opt-out toast)
9 improvements: retry requeue, send timeouts (30s), WA health check, warmup delay reduction, screenshot validation, multi-device parallel workers, text chunking, prefill boost, contact cache warmup
Production test fixes: Trust/Confiar dialog, callback 503 non-retryable, autocorrect disabled, generic BACK keyevent fallback
Monitoring infrastructure: docker-compose with Prometheus + Grafana, 4 provisioned dashboards
E2E validation: 18/19 callbacks 200 OK, 4 profiles tested, all senders working
Root capability audit: validated sendevent (0ms batch), /dev/uhid + CONFIG_UHID=y, sysfs thermal, SharedPrefs access

pendingTasks.task: P0: Eliminate hardcoded coordinates in send-engine.ts — replace with UIAutomator bounds
pendingTasks.notes: search icon at (624,172) and first result at (360,350) are POCO C71 specific. Must use UIAutomator XML bounds extraction like dismissDialogs() already does. Also diversify search behavior (name vs number, variable result position). Blocks multi-device/multi-model.

pendingTasks.task: P0: Adjust SendStrategy weights — prefill:10, search:50, typing:40
pendingTasks.notes: Prefill (wa.me?text=MSG) generates zero typing indicator — strongest automation fingerprint. Reduce to 10%, increase search (no wa.me) and typing (generates partial typing indicator). Depends on P0 coord fix for search to work on any device.

pendingTasks.task: P1: SharedPrefs root fallback in wa-account-mapper
pendingTasks.notes: Content provider is primary (works without root). Add su -c cp SharedPrefs fallback when run-as fails. Low effort, defensive.

/dev/uhid confirmed on POCO C71 (CONFIG_UHID=y). Need to: compile minimal UHID creator binary (C, ARM), push to device, create virtual keyboard, test key events trigger typing indicator in WhatsApp. UHID typing is ~7x slower than input text (30s vs 4s per msg). Must migrate ALL input text calls (typeMessage + openViaSearch). scrcpy has reference implementation.

Validated: su -c with inline sendevents = 0ms (vs 77ms for input tap). Touchscreen is /dev/input/event3. Supported ABS codes: 57 (tracking_id), 53 (position_x), 54 (position_y), 48 (touch_major). NO pressure sensor. Must use only supported codes.
Create contacts hours/days before first message. Requires pipeline change on both Oralsin and Dispatch sides. Medium-high effort.

pendingTasks.task: P3: WA auto-update control via pm disable
pendingTasks.notes: Operational, not code. pm disable-user com.android.vending on device connect.

architecturalDecisions.decision: Sendevent batch over individual su -c calls for tap injection
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Individual su -c per sendevent = 991ms. Single su -c with inline sendevents = 0ms. Batch is faster than input tap (77ms).

architecturalDecisions.decision: UHID keyboard preferred over input text for typing
architecturalDecisions.rationale: input text bypasses IME → no typing indicator → HIGH detection risk. UHID triggers proper InputConnection.commitText() → natural typing indicator. /dev/uhid confirmed available on POCO C71.

architecturalDecisions.decision: Strategy weights shift: prefill:10, search:50, typing:40
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Prefill (wa.me?text=MSG) generates ZERO typing indicator — strongest fingerprint. Search avoids wa.me entirely. Typing generates partial indicator.

architecturalDecisions.decision: Multi-device parallel workers via Promise.allSettled per device
architecturalDecisions.rationale: Single global workerRunning boolean → per-device Set. Each device processes independently. Already implemented.
alternatives: Sequential device processing (old — wastes second device)

architecturalDecisions.rationale: Oralsin returns 503 for orphaned callbacks (no ContactSchedule). Retrying won't help. Break immediately to avoid 230 wasted requests.
alternatives: Retry all non-2xx (old — caused 10x retry storm)

knownBugs.workaround: 1 pre-existing test failure, unrelated to our changes

knownBugs.issue: Uncommitted UI changes (device-info, live-screen, oralsin-*, shell-terminal) from prior sessions
knownBugs.workaround: Pre-existing staged changes, not part of hardening v2. Safe to ignore or commit separately.

established: WorkerOrchestrator.tickDevice() per-device with devicesRunning Set lock
Optional deps in constructors (SendEngine has 8 optional params — recorder, screenshotPolicy, contactCache, mediaSender)
SenderHealth emits sender:quarantined/released via optional DispatchEmitter
AuditLogger.log() with before/after state for all CRUD operations
EventRecorder.record() for append-only message trace events
dismissDialogs() pattern: regex match text in UIAutomator XML → extract bounds → tap center
su -c for root operations via adbkit shell (quoting is tricky — use separate calls for complex operations)
avoid: Hardcoded screen coordinates (624,172 and 360,350 in openViaSearch) — use UIAutomator bounds
prefill method for short messages — generates zero typing indicator
Individual su -c per sendevent — use batch inline
Complex piped commands via su in adbkit shell — write to /sdcard/ first then read

modifiedFiles: 30+ commits on feat/anti-ban-scaling since plan creation
